sentence: Subjective:

HPI: The patient is a 25-year-old male who presents with a several-week history of severe pain in the right upper quadrant of his abdomen, which is described as a squeezing pain that comes and goes, accompanied by nausea and vomiting.
He reports that the pain is worse after eating, particularly fatty foods, and rates the pain an 8 out of 10.
He has a history of type 2 diabetes, which he has managed with diet and exercise, including a keto diet, resulting in a 25-pound weight loss over the past 3 months.
He has a family history of gallbladder disease, with both parents having had their gallbladders removed.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of type 2 diabetes, which he has been managing with diet and exercise.
He also has a family history of gallbladder disease, with both parents having had their gallbladders removed.
Review of systems: The patient reports experiencing low-grade fevers and nausea, but no vomiting.
He also reports feeling nauseated and experiencing a low-grade fever when the pain is severe.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's blood pressure is 128/88, respiratory rate is 16, pulse rate is 68, and oxygen saturation is 99% on room air.
Physical exam: The patient has positive bowel sounds, a soft and nondistended abdomen, and slight guarding in the right upper quadrant.
The patient is positive for Murphy's sign, but does not exhibit peritoneal signs.
The common bile duct size is within normal limits at 4.1 mm.
Test Results: The abdominal ultrasound shows multiple gallstones present in the gallbladder and mild thickening of the gallbladder wall.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has mild cholecystitis with gallstones.
Problem list: Gallstones; Gallbladder disease; Type 2 diabetes.
Plan:
I recommended a laparoscopic cholecystectomy to remove the gallbladder, which is scheduled for next week.
The patient will need to avoid high-fat foods after surgery and eat foods high in fiber.
I will see the patient in a week to check on their progress and release them to return to normal activities.
Follow-up: The patient will see the patient in a week for a follow-up appointment and to have the gallbladder removed.
